hero.title: Building bright futures
hero.description: At IEX we shape well-rounded individuals with critical thinking, strong values, and the tools to stand out in a constantly changing world.

1.alt: Children taking part in class with their teacher

2.alt: Elementary students collaborating in the classroom

3.alt: Young students working together with laptops

4.alt: Middle school students in the school hallways

why.title: Why choose IEX?
why.excellenceBody: Our model blends academic rigor, social-emotional growth, and experiences that spark curiosity. Every student receives personalized support to reach their full potential.

arts.title: Arts workshops
arts.alt: Students painting and creating in an art workshop

classroom.title: Active classroom
classroom.alt: Students participating in a dynamic lesson

science.title: Science and lab
science.alt: Students experimenting in the science lab

preescolar.title: Preschool
preescolar.description: A playful, safe first school experience that sparks curiosity and a love of learning.

primaria.title: Elementary
primaria.description: Strong foundations in literacy, math, and science through collaborative projects.

secundaria.title: Middle school
secundaria.description: We strengthen autonomy, study habits, and life skills in a supportive setting.

bachillerato.title: High school
bachillerato.description: Preparation for entrance exams and university challenges with an analytical focus.

hero.imageAlt: Young children exploring and learning in a preschool classroom
hero.title: First years filled with play, routines, and care
hero.description: Safe environments where language, motor skills, and community grow with specialized teachers and close partnership with every family.
hero.meta: Multi-sensory classroom · Early stimulation · Exploratory English

propuesta.title: Educational approach
propuesta.lead: We begin with meaningful play, clear routines, and experiences that spark curiosity—without rushing development.
propuesta.body: Short sequences use concrete materials, music, rhyme, and storytime. Each week includes assembly, outdoor exploration, and small projects tied to children’s daily lives.
propuesta.imageAlt: Children painting and playing in the classroom

ambientes.title: Spaces and materials
ambientes.lead: Bright rooms, themed corners, and safe materials invite guided exploration and independence.
ambientes.body: We rotate stations for art, building, reading, and dramatic play. Materials sit at child height to support decision-making, order, and respect for the environment—always supervised.

familia.title: Partnership with families
familia.lead: Brief, steady communication: journals, interviews, and listening spaces.
familia.body: Learning support walks families through adaptation and shares practical tips. We celebrate growth together and align sleep, nutrition, and routines between home and school.
familia.imageAlt: Teacher greeting parents at the school entrance

cta.title: Learn about start dates and a guided tour
cta.body: We explain the admissions process for early years and answer questions about groups and schedules.

hero.imageAlt: Elementary students taking part in class
hero.title: Strong foundations in literacy, math, and citizenship
hero.description: We strengthen logical thinking, reading comprehension, and values in an active setting with responsible technology and collaborative projects.
hero.meta: Grade-level follow-up · Formative assessment · Optional clubs

bases.title: Literacy and mathematics
bases.lead: Daily reading routines, contextual problems, and oral expression workshops.
bases.body: We use varied texts and visual strategies to understand and argue. In math we emphasize number sense, estimation, and collaborative problem solving before algorithms.
bases.imageAlt: Elementary students reading in a circle with their teacher

proyectos.title: Projects and teamwork
proyectos.lead: Science, art, and language merge in multi-week challenges with a public showcase.
proyectos.body: Teams rotate roles, document progress, and reflect on learning. Students practice communication, empathy, and shared responsibility suited to their age.
proyectos.imageAlt: Students presenting a project with posters

tecnologia.title: Technology with purpose
tecnologia.lead: Tablets and digital tools follow clear lesson plans and digital citizenship agreements.
tecnologia.body: We highlight critical search, simple design, and respectful device use. Screen-free periods balance focus and wellbeing.
tecnologia.imageAlt: Children using tablets in pairs with teacher guidance

cta.title: Planning the move to elementary?
cta.body: Request information, review the plan by grade, and check documentation requirements.

hero.imageAlt: Middle school students in a group activity
hero.title: Autonomy, study habits, and guidance for adolescence
hero.description: We blend academic rigor, STEAM, sports, and career guidance so students build confidence about their path.

steam.title: Science, arts, and technology
steam.lead: Guided experiments, prototypes, and an internal interdisciplinary project fair.
steam.body: We grow skills like hypothesis, trial and error, and presenting results. Teachers connect topics to issues close to students’ realities.
steam.imageAlt: Students in a science lab handling materials

vocacional.title: Guidance and wellbeing
vocacional.lead: Tutoring links personal goals, study habits, and first vocational choices.
vocacional.body: Workshops on social-emotional skills, time management, and exam stress. We support family conversations at key moments.
vocacional.imageAlt: Counselor talking with students in the hallway

comunidad.title: Leadership and service
comunidad.lead: Student teams, debate, and projects that serve the school community.
comunidad.body: We foster respectful voice, peer mediation, and celebration of diversity. Sports, music, and short volunteering opportunities are available.
comunidad.imageAlt: Teens organizing a cultural activity together

cta.title: Plan the transition to middle school
cta.body: Review the curriculum, lab schedules, and club options for the upcoming year.

hero.imageAlt: High school students studying for university prep
hero.title: Serious preparation for university and professional life
hero.description: Mock exams, research, mentoring, and targeted support for entrance tests—without losing holistic growth.
hero.meta: Periodic mock exams · Advising · Scholarships and partnerships

excelencia.title: High expectations with support
excelencia.lead: Semester planning, transparent rubrics, and scheduled help before major assessments.
excelencia.body: You receive actionable feedback in math, reading, and science. We prioritize reasoning and written argument—core skills for selection exams.
excelencia.imageAlt: Students in class taking focused notes

investigacion.title: Research projects
investigacion.lead: A short monograph or applied project with faculty advising and fair-style presentation.
investigacion.body: You learn to cite sources, synthesize evidence, and speak clearly. It previews the university-level work ahead after IEX.

universidad.title: Bridge to university
universidad.lead: Exam-style mocks, career-choice workshops, and alumni talks.
universidad.body: We map strengths and realistic options. If you apply for institutional scholarships, we help with requirements and deadlines without losing academic pace.

cta.title: Take the next step toward university
cta.body: Review the mock-exam calendar and high school admissions profile.

hero.imageAlt: Children using immersive technology to learn in the classroom
hero.title: English, augmented reality, robotics, and coding
hero.description: Students practice English through AR glasses, build and program robots, and grow computational thinking with hands-on, playful projects.
hero.meta: AR + English · Robotics · Creative code · Teamwork

arIngles.title: English with augmented reality
arIngles.lead: Digital scenes and characters support pronunciation, vocabulary, and listening.
arIngles.body: With AR glasses, learners practice dialogues in simulated contexts, play language challenges, and get immediate feedback—building confidence to speak English in a living language-lab atmosphere.
arIngles.imageAlt: Child wearing augmented reality glasses during an activity

robotica.title: Educational robotics
robotica.lead: Build, test, and tune: basic mechanics, sensors, and teamwork.
robotica.body: Students solve simple challenges with classroom kits—line following, obstacle avoidance, or a team mission. The room works like a makerspace to test ideas and celebrate every prototype that runs.
robotica.imageAlt: Hand adjusting an educational robotic arm in a workshop

programacion.title: Programming and logical thinking
programacion.lead: Sequences, conditions, and loops become visual before students write code.
programacion.body: We move from guided block-based activities to small programs that drive robots or simple animations. The focus is algorithmic thinking, patient debugging, and respect for peers when sharing projects.
programacion.imageAlt: Screen showing code and an educational programming resource

marquee.ariaLabel: Gallery of EPIC program moments with technology and active learning
marquee.title: EPIC in the classroom
marquee.lead: Workshops, prototypes, and collaboration—a scrolling strip of images from the program.

cta.title: Interested in EPIC for your child?
cta.body: Ask about availability, recommended ages, and how EPIC fits with IEX’s academic offer.

model.title: Holistic educational model
model.description: We integrate academics, values, sports, arts, and technology so each student builds a clear vision of their future—and the skills to achieve it.

steps.descripcion: Schedule a visit or request details online or by phone.
We learn about the student and family expectations.
Academic evaluation aligned with the requested level.
You receive feedback and the next step in the process.
Submit documents and secure your place.
Onboarding and start of the school year.

cta.title: Start your child's future today!
cta.subtitle: We guide you through every admissions step with clarity and care.

partners.title: Partners and certifications
partners.subtitle: Institutional backing, official recognition, and partnerships that strengthen IEX’s educational offer.
partners.brandLine: Identity and track record behind every stage of learning
partners.lead: Our model rests on clear processes, pedagogical follow-up, and ties with bodies that certify quality and broaden opportunities for students and families.
partners.body: We work with educational institutions, certifiers, and professional networks so we can update practices, offer programs aligned with recognized standards, and prepare students for a global environment.
partners.closing: Families’ trust is built through transparency, results, and partnerships that show our commitment to excellence.

hero.title: Science & technology fair 2026
hero.description: Student projects, experiments, and prototypes from all levels. Families and community welcome.

pastSection.title: Past events
pastSection.subtitle: Moments that shape our school life: celebrations, competitions, culture, and service.

feriaCiencias.title: Science fair
feriaCiencias.caption: STEM projects and invention showcase · March 2025

olimpiada.title: Sports olympics
olimpiada.caption: Interschool athletics and team sports · November 2024

semanaCultural.title: Cultural week
semanaCultural.caption: Theater, music, and dance at the auditorium · October 2024

debate.title: Debate tournament
debate.caption: High school league · Civic education · May 2024

arte.title: Young art expo
arte.caption: Student painting and sculpture show · April 2024

lectura.title: Reading marathon
lectura.caption: Book day and reading clubs · April 2024

servicio.title: Service day
servicio.caption: Community project and solidarity drive · March 2024

commitment.title: Cultural commitment
commitment.imageAlt: Students in an arts and cultural activity at school
commitment.lead: At IEX we believe holistic education includes the arts, identity, and respect for diversity. Our cultural events and projects help every student find their voice, collaborate with others, and connect thoughtfully with the community.
commitment.body: We promote reading, music, theater, exhibitions, and service days as part of the curriculum and school life. In this way we nurture sensitive, critical, and caring citizens aligned with the school’s values and a genuine social commitment beyond the classroom.

grandFinal.ariaLabel: End-of-year grand event with a stage production in the auditorium
grandFinal.title: End‑of‑year gala: a stage production that brings the whole community together
grandFinal.description: At the end of the school year, the auditorium transforms: students across levels take the stage to celebrate learning, discipline, and teamwork through a live production.
bullets: Recognizes students’ effort and growth throughout the year
Builds stage confidence, expression, and collaboration
Brings families and the community together in a memorable moment
Closes the year with pride, identity, and belonging

classroom.alt: Students in class taking part in the lesson

teamwork.alt: Students collaborating on a school project

hallway.alt: Middle school students in the hallway

sports.alt: Student sports team on the court

contact.title: Contact
contact.partnersHint: See the «Partners and certifications» section for certifications and partners.

hero.imageAlt: Architecture and built spaces as facilities reference
hero.title: Spaces designed for every stage
hero.description: Classrooms, shared areas, and environments built to learn, explore, and connect—safely.

sections.lead: Spaces for preschool, elementary, middle school, and high school—bright, organized, and ready to learn.
sections.body: Each classroom supports participation, reading, teamwork, and focused practice. Layout and materials adapt by age to support development at every stage.

imagenes.alt: Hallway or interior area of a building

sections.lead: Spaces to experiment, code, and learn with structure: science, robotics, and digital resources.
sections.body: Hands-on work strengthens critical thinking. In labs, students tackle guided challenges, record results, and prototype, document, and present projects.

sections.lead: Courtyards and open spaces to move, breathe, and connect—safely.
sections.body: Break time also teaches: respectful community, active play, and healthy habits. Shared areas support smooth transitions between classes, clubs, and activities.

imagenes.alt: Outdoor building space with natural light
Courtyard or plaza reference for gathering

sections.lead: Play zones to explore with confidence: movement, coordination, and discovery.
sections.body: In preschool, space is part of learning. Play structures and corners support motor skills, imagination, and community, with clear routines and supervision.

sections.lead: A comfortable place to refuel and connect.
sections.body: The cafeteria supports healthy habits and community. It’s a meeting point for students across levels, with clear schedules that keep the day flowing smoothly.

imagenes.alt: Indoor area with tables as cafeteria reference

hero.imageAlt: School services and support for students and families
hero.title: Support that helps every day
hero.description: Services and guidance that strengthen the school experience with clarity and care.

menuItems: Monday: vegetable broth, baked chicken milanese with mash, seasonal fruit, and water
Tuesday: whole‑wheat pasta with tomato and vegetable sauce, mixed salad, sugar‑free gelatin, and water
Wednesday: red rice, steamed fish with lemon, steamed vegetables, fruit, and water
Thursday: vegetable soup, crispy tacos (potato or chicken), refried beans, salad, and water
Friday: corn cream soup, stuffed chicken breast with salad, fruit, and water
comedor.nutritionBody: A well‑planned school cafeteria supports focus, steady energy, and lifelong habits. We emphasize balanced preparation, variety across food groups, age‑appropriate portions, and good hydration. When possible, we limit added sugars and ultra‑processed items, prioritizing home‑style cooking and menu visibility for families.

extensionHorario.scheduleHighlight: By program, hours are extended from 2:00 p.m. to 5:00 p.m.
extensionHorario.scheduleBody: This service helps families balance schedules while keeping students safe: extra time is supervised with age‑appropriate, playful, formative activities.
scheduleBullets: Clear group routines for the extended window
Coordination with teachers and student-life teams
Communication on arrivals, departures, and authorized guardians

extraParagraphs: When siblings are in different grades, logistics multiply—timelines, messages, and paperwork can pile up. We help centralize information and guide families to reduce friction.
extraBullets: Guidance to align calendars across levels
Contact points for academic and administrative questions
Support during transitions (new level or new school year)

openDoorBullets: Scheduled conversations with academic and administrative coordination
Clear explanation of policies, assessment, and family communication
Tours of key campus areas (per institutional calendar)
Guidance on paperwork, payments, scholarships, and documentation when applicable
Answers on community life, support channels, and reporting
Follow‑up with written agreements when needed

extensionHorario.alt: Classroom or study space reference for extended hours

diferentesNiveles.alt: Hallway or transition area reference for children in different levels

puertasAbiertas.alt: Entrance or facade reference for an open-door school

services.title: Cafeteria
services.description: A comfortable place to refuel, with clear time slots for each group.
bullets: Comfortable environment to eat and connect
Routines that promote order and hygiene

services.title: Extended hours
services.description: Support for families who need flexibility: supervised time and activities on campus.

services.title: Children in different levels or grades
services.description: We help families organize when siblings are in different levels, with coordination and communication that makes the day simpler.

services.title: Open-door school
services.description: We believe in transparency and a strong family partnership: spaces to ask questions, support each process, and build trust.
bullets: A culture of community and closeness

hero.imageAlt: IEX students sharing their school experience
hero.title: School life in students’ own words
hero.description: Perspectives from different levels: what students value about IEX, the classroom, and the community.

intro.title: Student voices
intro.lead: Each level has its rhythm; here we share representative thoughts from preschool through high school.
intro.body: These are opinion-style testimonials (not verbatim interviews): they reflect common motivations without exposing real personal data.

preescolar.name: Lucía M.
preescolar.imageAlt: Illustrative portrait of a preschool student
preescolar.opinionLead: I like coming to school because we play, sing, and try new things without being scared.
preescolar.opinionBody: In class we have purposeful play and story time. I feel cared for when the teacher explains calmly and when classmates share. My favorite part is going outside and learning with my hands.
highlights: Clear routines that help me feel safe
Activities with colors, music, and movement
Teachers who listen when I have questions

primaria.name: Diego R.
primaria.imageAlt: Illustrative portrait of an elementary student
primaria.opinionLead: I learn things I can explain to my family at dinner.
primaria.opinionBody: We do group projects and sometimes we disagree—and we still have to agree. I like reading aloud because I’m less afraid of mistakes. Teachers are demanding but also help when we get stuck.
highlights: Reading and math with real-life examples
Projects where everyone contributes ideas
A space where I can ask without feeling judged

secundaria.name: Valentina S.
secundaria.imageAlt: Illustrative portrait of a middle school student
secundaria.opinionLead: You learn to get organized because there are many subjects—but there’s room for hobbies too.
secundaria.opinionBody: I value that it’s not only memorizing: sometimes we research, debate, and present. Clubs give me a break without dropping sports. With friends, we feel peer life improves when rules are clear and there’s mediation.
highlights: Tutoring that helps ground personal goals
Lab work and challenges that connect theory to practice
Spaces for sports and healthy community life

bachillerato.name: Mateo G.
bachillerato.imageAlt: Illustrative portrait of a high school student
bachillerato.opinionLead: I stayed because I feel prepared for something bigger than one test.
bachillerato.opinionBody: The pace is demanding, but feedback is honest. I like when teachers ask us to argue, not just repeat formulas. Practice exams reduced fear of the unknown and clarified my university plan.
highlights: Mock exams and follow-up without shaming anyone who struggles
Projects that teach research and stronger writing
Close mentoring for university preparation decisions

hero.imageAlt: Parents connected to the IEX community
hero.title: Alumni who now educate as parents
hero.description: Parents who studied at IEX share why they trust the school for their children.

intro.title: Why we return as parents
intro.lead: Being an alum changes your lens: you value long-term consistency and human care across the years.
intro.body: These are representative testimonials about common motivations; they don’t replace official admissions information or individual guarantees.

ana.name: Ana P.
ana.imageAlt: Illustrative portrait of an alumna parent
ana.opinionLead: I came back with my children because I know what it feels like to grow up here.
ana.opinionBody: What I value most is the blend of academic rigor and guidance. I received support at key moments and today I appreciate clear channels with coordination. I’m not looking for a school with no homework—I want one that teaches responsibility.
highlights: I trust the continuity across school levels
Communication home is concrete—not just formalities
Values and community life are treated seriously, not as slogans

carlos.name: Carlos E.
carlos.imageAlt: Illustrative portrait of an alumnus parent
carlos.opinionLead: I chose IEX again because I want strong foundations and judgment for my kids.
carlos.opinionBody: As a student I learned to study with method more than cramming overnight. I see the same now: projects and rubrics help students understand where they stand. A safe environment and teachers who take ownership matter to me.
highlights: Emphasis on study habits and independent work
Projects and presentations that grow communication
An orderly setting with clear community rules

mariana.name: Mariana L.
mariana.imageAlt: Illustrative portrait of an alumna parent
mariana.opinionLead: To me IEX means community—it’s not just walk in, walk out.
mariana.opinionBody: What convinces me as a mom is follow-up when a child needs extra support or when peers clash. You can tell when a school invests in counseling instead of leaving everything to chance. The alumni network also builds confidence—you belong.
highlights: Support when motivation dips or conflicts appear
Activities that involve family without overloading the calendar
Belonging I’ve felt since I was a student

hero.imageAlt: Students participating in an after-school workshop on campus
hero.title: Learning that continues after the bell
hero.description: Afternoon workshops to explore art, science, sports, and technology with specialist guidance in a safe, motivating environment.
hero.meta: Enrollment by term · Limited spots per group

horario.title: Schedule and rhythm
horario.lead: Workshops run right after the regular school day, respecting breaks and on-campus transition times.
horario.body: Each experience is designed in 60–90 minute blocks, with materials included and a supply list shared on day one so families can plan pickup and drop-off confidently.
horario.imageAlt: Art room with easels and students painting

oferta.title: What we offer
oferta.lead: We blend artistic expression, STEAM labs, public speaking, educational robotics, and formative sports.
oferta.body: Content is refreshed each school year based on student interests and guest instructors. We share the catalog before registration so your family can decide together.
oferta.imageAlt: Students at a science fair with projects on tables

comunidad.title: Formation and community
comunidad.lead: Workshops strengthen shared responsibility, leadership, and teamwork beyond grades.
comunidad.body: We provide feedback with tutor teachers, open showcases for families, and closing celebrations that honor the process. The goal is lasting curiosity and consistency.
comunidad.imageAlt: Students applauding during a cultural presentation

video.title: Workshops and creativity at school
video.description: This reference video (TED talk in English—enable captions on YouTube if you like) explores fostering creativity in education, aligned with the spirit of our afternoon workshops.
video.caption: External content hosted on YouTube. You can replace the embed ID in code with IEX-owned media.

available.title: Available workshops
available.subtitle: Categories offered each school year; coordinators confirm age group, eligibility, and capacity.

futbol.title: Soccer
futbol.description: Formative training focused on conditioning, teamwork, and fair play. Warm-ups, drills, and small-sided games.

arte.title: Art
arte.description: Visual arts studio: drawing, painting, and mixed media. Short exhibitions at the end of each term.

teatro.title: Theater
teatro.description: Voice, movement, and dramatic reading. Students produce short scenes and a small ensemble piece each cycle.

ajedrez.title: Chess
ajedrez.description: Openings, tactics, and timed games. Logic, patience, and respect for opponents.

robotica.title: Robotics
robotica.description: Educational kits, simple sensors, and staged coding challenges. Collaborative STEAM mindset.

schedule.title: Afternoon workshop schedule
schedule.subtitle: Sample times for the school year; confirm with student-life coordinators if venues change.
schedule.caption: Afternoon workshops table: days, times, and location.
schedule.note: Times may shift for assemblies, special events, or facility maintenance. Supply lists and internal guidelines are shared at registration.

hero.imageAlt: Teachers and students collaborating in the classroom
hero.title: The educators who guide each stage with rigor and care
hero.description: Meet a few voices from IEX: their role, what they lead in class, and how they connect with families.

intro.title: Teachers at the heart of the project
intro.lead: Every level has educators who review practice together, share strategies, and follow each group’s progress closely.
intro.body: Here we highlight representative profiles—not a full roster, but a sample of typical responsibilities and activities alongside students.

andrea.name: Andrea Castillo, M.Ed.
andrea.imageAlt: Portrait of Andrea Castillo, IEX teacher
andrea.activitiesLead: She designs sequences where the classroom becomes a studio: inquiry, peer assessment, and critical use of sources.
andrea.activitiesBody: She supports elementary and middle school faculty in improvement cycles; with students she facilitates short projects with public showcases to practice argumentation and teamwork.
highlights: Cross-grade reading club with student reading coaches.
Feedback rounds with students after each STEM project.

rodrigo.name: Rodrigo Hernandez, M.Ed.
rodrigo.imageAlt: Portrait of Rodrigo Hernandez, IEX tutor
rodrigo.activitiesLead: He coordinates tutoring that blends study habits, wellbeing, and communication with home.
rodrigo.activitiesBody: Biweekly class sessions, one-to-one listening spaces, and short workshops on time management and exam stress. He partners with counseling when a family needs a shared plan.
highlights: Facilitated restorative circles among peers in middle school.
Brief family updates with observable wins and next steps.

sofia.name: Sofia Mendez, B.Ed.
sofia.imageAlt: Portrait of Sofia Mendez, IEX English teacher
sofia.activitiesLead: She drives communicative English through oral projects, drama, and read-alouds.
sofia.activitiesBody: In elementary and middle school she leads deliberate practice: pronunciation, short presentations, and language portfolios. Each term she hosts a mini-talks fair where students present to families.
highlights: Student-teacher role swaps in 10-minute micro-lessons.
Shared rubrics to self-assess pronunciation and coherence.

hero.imageAlt: School building entrance and campus access
hero.title: A clear path to becoming part of IEX
hero.description: We guide you from first contact through official welcome with timely information and spaces to understand our educational model.
hero.meta: Guided visits by appointment · Assessments by level

admission.stepsIntro: These are the typical stages; admissions will share current schedules and paperwork.

informes.title: First contact and visit
informes.lead: Book a tour or request information to see facilities, the model, and availability by level.
informes.body: We answer questions about tuition, calendar, transportation, and services. If you cannot visit in person, we offer an online session with the same level of detail.
informes.imageAlt: Family with student at the school reception

evaluacion.title: Student assessment
evaluacion.lead: We meet the family and apply a diagnostic assessment suited to age and requested grade.
evaluacion.body: Results guide enrollment decisions and early support needs. You always receive feedback on strengths and recommended next pedagogical steps.
evaluacion.imageAlt: Student taking a written assessment with support nearby

integracion.title: Welcome and first weeks
integracion.lead: After results, enrollment and an integration plan are finalized.
integracion.body: We schedule welcome activities, tutor assignment, and support with materials. We want the transition to feel positive for students and families.
integracion.imageAlt: New students during a group integration activity

hero.imageAlt: Students studying in the school library
hero.title: Scholarships that recognize effort and talent
hero.description: Options for families seeking continuity in quality education with clear criteria and personal guidance.
hero.meta: Call for applications each term · Institutional scholarship committee

filosofia.title: Program philosophy
filosofia.lead: We believe in equitable access without giving up academic excellence and IEX’s holistic model.
filosofia.body: Awards combine academic merit, talent in specific areas, or verified financial need. Confidentiality of family information is a priority.
filosofia.imageAlt: Books and notebooks on a study desk

criterios.title: Criteria and paperwork
criterios.lead: Formal request, recent school records, motivation letter, and in some cases a committee interview.
criterios.body: We provide a checklist to avoid unnecessary steps. Support percentages depend on holistic review and budget availability for the term.
criterios.imageAlt: Documents and study plan on a desk

acompanamiento.title: Support for scholarship students
acompanamiento.lead: Benefits are tied to academic commitment, conduct, and school participation.
acompanamiento.body: We review progress each term to celebrate wins and, if needed, adjust learning supports. Families receive guidance to plan ahead within the program.
acompanamiento.imageAlt: Student and mentor reviewing progress on a tablet

ctaBand.title: Ready to start your application?
ctaBand.body: Contact us and we will send the official form and answer questions about deadlines and current requirements.

buzon.title: Complaints and suggestions box
buzon.description: A confidential channel to report situations, suggest improvements, and strengthen school wellbeing.

footer.about: We develop leaders with critical thinking, values, and social commitment in a safe, caring environment.
